data.title: YIJIA GROUP CORP. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
YIJIA GROUP CORP., a company engaged in consulting advisory services and healthcare product marketing, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its revenue streams, geographical performance, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue, net: $103,899,000, a decrease of 4% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to changes in customer location and segment performance.
  • Gross profit: $68,781,000, with a gross profit margin of 66%, slightly down from 67% in the prior year.
  • Loss before income tax: $(32,440,000), an improvement from the $(45,414,000) loss in the previous year, reflecting better cost management.
  • Net loss: $(46,301,000), compared to a net loss of $(55,211,000) in the same period last year, indicating a reduction in overall losses.
  • Loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.00), consistent with the previous year, reflecting the unchanged number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in two business segments: Consulting Service Segment and Healthcare Segment. For the three months ended July 31, 2025, the Consulting Service Segment generated $4,225 in revenue, while the Healthcare Segment generated $99,674.
  • Geographical Performance: During the three months ended July 31, 2025, all revenue was generated from customers located in the United States, whereas in the same period in 2024, all revenue was generated from customers in Hong Kong.
  • Operational Focus: The company continues to provide consulting advisory services in management, business, accounting, and finance, alongside marketing and supplying healthcare products.
  • Future Outlook: The company is actively seeking other opportunities to potentially increase profits in 2025, indicating a strategic focus on growth and expansion.

SEC Filing: YIJIA GROUP CORP. [ YJGJ ] - 10-Q - Sep. 12, 2025

data.title: Trio Petroleum Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Trio Petroleum Corp, a company engaged in the exploration and production of oil and gas, has released its Form 10-Q report for the fiscal quarter ended July 31, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial performance and operational progress, reflecting the company's ongoing efforts to enhance its production capabilities and profitability.

Financial Highlights

Trio Petroleum Corp reported notable financial performance for the three months ended July 31, 2025:

  • Revenues: $192,395, up from $63,052 in the same period in 2024, indicating improved operational progress.
  • Gross Profit: $93,906, compared to $63,052 in the same period in 2024, showing enhanced profitability.
  • Loss from Operations: $(675,026), a significant reduction from $(1,510,190) in the same period in 2024, demonstrating a reduction in operating losses.
  • Net Loss: $(1,386,723), compared to $(2,178,571) in the same period in 2024, indicating a narrowing of net losses.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss per Common Share: $(0.17), compared to $(0.87) in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved per-share performance.

Business Highlights

Trio Petroleum Corp's operational performance for the fiscal quarter ended July 31, 2025, includes several key developments:

  • Geographical Performance: Operations in Saskatchewan, Canada, have shown sustained and improved revenue performance, reflecting continued operational progress in the region.
  • Sales Units: As of July 31, 2025, the company had seven wells producing oil, all located in the newly acquired Saskatchewan property, indicating an expansion in production capacity.
  • New Production Launches: The company recognized initial revenues from its Saskatchewan assets during the fiscal quarter ended April 30, 2025, marking a significant milestone in its operational expansion.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to add the reserve value of its Saskatchewan fields to its reserve report after further observation and review of oil production, which will subsequently allow for the estimation of necessary depreciation, depletion, and amortization for these wells.

SEC Filing: Trio Petroleum Corp [ TPET ] - 10-Q - Sep. 12, 2025

data.title: Fyntechnical Innovations Inc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fyntechnical Innovations Inc., formerly known as SMC Entertainment, Inc., has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The company, which focuses on developing AI-enabled wealth management technology, has shown significant financial and operational developments, particularly following its recent acquisitions and strategic integrations.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: Fyntechnical Innovations Inc. reported revenue of $24,499 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024. This revenue was generated from management fees from the newly acquired Bateau Asset Management Pty, Ltd.

Net Income: The company achieved a net income of $6,933,969 for the six months ended June 30, 2025, a significant turnaround from a net loss of $14,463,599 for the same period in 2024. The net income was primarily driven by a gain on extinguishment of debt amounting to $8,308,360.

Net (loss) income per share – basic: $0.00 for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.01) for the same period in 2024.

Net (loss) income per share – diluted: $0.00 for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.01) for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Fyntechnical Innovations Inc. focuses on developing AI-enabled wealth management technology. The company completed the acquisition of Fyniti Global Equities EBT Inc. in April 2023, enhancing its fintech capabilities.

Acquisition of Bateau Asset Management: In January 2025, the company acquired Bateau Asset Management Pty, Ltd., an Australian investment manager, to expand its investment management services. This acquisition is expected to integrate Bateau's absolute-return investment philosophy and multi-manager approach into the company's offerings.

Revenue Segments: Revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, was primarily generated from management fees through the newly acquired Bateau Asset Management Pty, Ltd.

Operational Integration: The company is consolidating its current and legacy technologies under the FYNN AI platform to streamline product offerings and reduce development redundancies, aiming for faster deployment of future features.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue issuing additional equity and debt securities to meet its capital requirements and support its business operations. The integration of Bateau is expected to enhance the company's service offerings and operational efficiency.

Geographical Performance: Bateau Asset Management, based in Australia with offices in Singapore, contributes to the company's international presence and revenue generation through its management fees.

Management's Discussion: The management highlighted the increase in general and administrative expenses due to the integration of Bateau, while also noting a decrease in consulting expenses. The company is focused on leveraging its acquisitions to drive future growth.

SEC Filing: Fyntechnical Innovations Inc [ FYNN ] - 10-Q - Sep. 12, 2025

data.title: UBS Loses More Financial Advisors, This Time to Morgan Stanley — Barrons.com
data.description:
By David Wignall

UBS Wealth Management continues to suffer from retention issues, as two advisors who were part of a team managing $2 billion defected to Morgan Stanley.

Advisors Randy Wiggs and Michael Simone registered with Morgan Stanley on Aug. 22, according to BrokerCheck, a database maintained by industry self-regulator Finra. The duo are industry veterans: both began their careers at Merrill Lynch in the 1990s, before joining UBS in 2008.

At UBS, the duo worked alongside brokers Paul Lee and Ryan Lindgren at a practice called Legacy Partners, according to an archived version of UBS' website. Both Lee and Lindgren are still at UBS. According to information provided to Barron's, Legacy Partners oversaw $2 billion. AdvisorHub first reported on the move.

Wiggs and Simone are calling their new practice Legacy Wealth Group. They will operate four-days per week out of Chicago, and one day per week out of Barrington, Ill., according to a person familiar with the matter.

A representative for Morgan Stanley didn't immediately respond to a request for comment. A spokesman for UBS declined to comment.

Wiggs and Simone aren't the only advisors to have left UBS in recent months. The bank, which in November announced changes to its 2025 advisor compensation plan, has been suffering from a string of high-profile defections. Head count for the company's Americas wealth unit fell to 5,773 at the end of the second quarter, compared with 5,884 for the first quarter. The firm's head count figures include some advisors in Canada and Latin America.

Recent departures include a $1.3 billion team who left for JP Morgan Chase and three billion-dollar teams who decamped to RBC. Morgan Stanley has been poaching UBS advisors as well, including a $1.6 billion group in April and a $750 million group in June.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Alliance Laundry Holdings Principal Shareholder Is BDT & MSD Partners
TPG RE Finance Trust, Inc. Declares Cash Dividend on Common Stock
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 12, 2025--

TPG RE Finance Trust, Inc. ("TRTX" or the "Company") today announced that the Company's Board of Directors has declared a cash dividend of $0.24 per share of common stock for the third quarter of 2025. The dividend will be payable on October 24, 2025 to common stockholders of record as of September 26, 2025.

ABOUT TRTX

TRTX is a commercial real estate finance company that originates, acquires, and manages primarily first mortgage loans secured by institutional properties located in primary and select secondary markets in the United States. The Company is externally managed by TPG RE Finance Trust Management, L.P., a part of TPG Real Estate, which is the real estate investment platform of global alternative asset management firm TPG Inc. . For more information regarding TRTX, visit https://www.tpgrefinance.com/.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This press release contains "forward--looking statements" within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. These forward--looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties, including, without limitation, risks and uncertainties relating to: the performance of the Company's investments; global economic trends and economic conditions, including heightened inflation, slower growth or recession, changes to fiscal and monetary policy, higher interest rates, tariffs and international trade policies, stress to the commercial banking systems of the U.S. and Western Europe, labor shortages, currency fluctuations and challenges in global supply chains; the Company's ability to originate loans that are in the pipeline and under evaluation by the Company; financing needs and arrangements; and the risks, uncertainties and factors set forth under the heading "Risk Factors" in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, as such risk factors may be updated from time to time in the Company's periodic filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC"), which are accessible on the SEC's website at www.sec.gov. Forward-looking statements are generally identifiable by use of forward-looking terminology such as "may," "will, " "should," "potential," "intend," "expect," "endeavor," "seek," "anticipate," "estimate," "believe," "could," "project," "predict," "continue," "payable" or other similar words or expressions. Forward-looking statements are based on certain assumptions, discuss future expectations, describe existing or future plans and strategies, contain projections of results of operations, liquidity and/or financial condition or state other forward-looking information. Statements, among others, relating to the payment of dividends on a future date are forward-looking statements. The ability of TRTX to predict future events or conditions or their impact or the actual effect of existing or future plans or strategies is inherently uncertain. Although the Company believes that such forward-looking statements are based on reasonable assumptions, actual results and performance in the future could differ materially from those set forth in or implied by such forward-looking statements. You are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which reflect the Company's views only as of the date of this press release. data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1609 ET - Livestock futures close down, with most-active live cattle futures finishing down 1% to $2.3105 a pound and lean hog futures closing down 1.1% to 98 cents a pound. Weaker export sales were reported for both pork and beef in yesterday's weekly export sales report — with beef sales down 27% from the previous week to 12,100 metric tons and pork sales down 27% to 17,300 tons. Weak export figures are one source of pressure for futures, says ADM Investor Services in a note. September's WASDE report from the USDA showed that the agency expects beef prices to continue rising heading into 2026, while pork prices also climb. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)The report from Brazilian crop agency Conab showed an increased outlook for corn crops to 139.5 MMT from 137.0 MMT previously. "Estimates for South American Crops Grow — Market Talk," at 11:30 a.m. ET on Sept. 11, incorrectly said the outlook for corn was 139.7 MMT from 137.0 MMT previously.

1523 ET - Oil futures post weekly gains with geopolitical risk premium outweighing oversupply concerns as the Russia-Ukraine war continues unabated and an Israeli strike aimed at Hamas leaders in Qatar threatens to increase tensions in the Middle East. The market took in stride OPEC+ plans to continue unwinding output cuts in October, while another unexpected weekly U.S. crude stock build was seen as bearish. Additional U.K. sanctions against Russia and U.S. calls for Europe to hit India and China with tariffs for buying Russian oil gave prices a lift at the end of the week. WTI settles up 0.5% at $62.69 for a 1.3% weekly gain, and Brent rises 0.9% to $66.99, up 2.3% from a week ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1516 ET - Oil futures post weekly gains with geopolitical risk premium outweighing oversupply concerns as the Russia-Ukraine war continues unabated and an Israeli strike aimed at Hamas leaders in Qatar threatens to increase tensions in the Middle East. The market took in stride OPEC+ plans to continue unwinding output cuts in October, while another unexpected weekly U.S. crude stock build was seen as bearish. Additional U.K. sanctions against Russia and U.S. calls for Europe to hit India and China with tariffs for buying Russian oil gave prices a lift at the end of the week. WTI settles up 0.5% at $62.69 for a 1.3% weekly gain, and Brent rises 0.9% to $66.99, up 2.3% from a week ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1503 ET - Natural gas futures settle near flat to close out the week with a loss. A second straight above-average storage build put the brakes on rally attempts with the inventory surplus over the five-year average expanding to 188 Bcf from 173 Bcf the week before. "With the approach of the shoulder period, such a supply overhang will be providing a cushion against any unforeseen supply disruptions," Ritterbusch says in a note. "But for now, price support that usually emanates from this production slippage as seen this week, is being largely negated by a downswing in export activity." Nymex natural gas settles up 0.2% at $2.941/mmBtu, and is down 3.5% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com) (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1433 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. rose for a third consecutive week, increasing by two to 416, or 72 fewer than a year ago, Baker Hughes reports. Rigs directed at natural gas were unchanged this week at 118, up by 21 from a year earlier. The EIA expects drilling activity in 2026 to be more centered in natural gas-intensive regions given its forecasts for lower oil but higher natural gas prices. "We forecast that crude oil will trade at its lowest premium to natural gas since 2005," the EIA said in its latest Short Term Energy Outlook. "We expect decreases in natural gas produced as a by-product of oil directed drilling will offset increases in that produced by natural gas-directed drilling." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1428 ET - This afternoon's USDA WASDE report made only minor adjustments to the record corn forecasts introduced by the agency last month, keeping U.S. corn supply projections onerous. But CBOT corn futures are now leading grain futures higher in late trading, even with the USDA's projections not seen as supportive for prices. "On the surface yield, production and ending stocks numbers are bearish-especially with the market having rallied going into the report," says Joel Karlin of Ocean State Research. But traders seem to be instead taking the opportunity to cover on the net-short position seen in corn among managed-money firms--which Karlin says may be because traders believe that corn futures have established their low for the year. CBOT corn is up 2.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1318 ET - Production of wheat out of Russia and the European Union was lifted by the USDA in today's WASDE report. Production in the EU was lifted by 1.85 million metric tons to 140.1 million tons, and Russian production ticked up 1.5 million tons to 85 million tons. Ukrainian production was also hiked up 1 million tons, to 23 million tons. These higher world production figures put pressure on wheat futures after the report's release. "Definitely negative on wheat with the USDA ramping up the Black Sea and EU production," says Charlie Sernatinger of Marex. CBOT wheat is down 0.9%, while corn rises 0.2% and soybeans climb 0.7%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1309 ET - The USDA raises the forecast for U.S. cotton production in the current marketing year, "with no change to exports, consumption, imports, or stocks," the agency says in the September WASDE report. Output is estimated at 13.22 million bales, up from 13.21 million bales in the previous forecast. Ending stocks remain at 3.6 million bales. World production projection is raised to 117.68 million bales from 116.62 million bales, with stocks projection down to 73.14 from 73.91 million bales. The USDA says U.S. planted area has increased slightly. On the CBOT, cotton is slightly higher than yesterday, losing some steam after the WASDE report. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1256 ET - The Renewable Fuels Association says that the September WASDE report from the USDA underscores the need for more ethanol production in the U.S.--with the USDA now projecting even more corn to be produced by farmers this year. "The supply of corn following this year's harvest is expected to significantly overwhelm demand, leaving 2.1 billion bushels of surplus," the RFA says. "This report again underscores the urgent need to quickly open and expand markets for corn, including removing unnecessary barriers that constrain market opportunities for ethanol." The RFA says that allowing E15 -or motor vehicle fuel cut with 15% ethanol- would help offset the huge surplus. CBOT corn is up 0.2% in afternoon trading, this after initially falling on the report's release. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1246 ET - Beef will remain scarce and expensive into 2026, the USDA indicates in the September WASDE report, citing a slower rate of slaughter. Beef production in 2026 is projected at 25.49 billion pounds, little changed from the August estimate and down from a downwardly revised 25.83 billion pounds forecast for 2025. Steers price estimates for 2026 rise to $2.49 from $2.44 a pound. Pork production in 2026 remains at 28.38 billion pounds, up from downwardly revised 27.57 billion pounds 2025 estimate. Barrows and gilts 2026 price estimate is kept at 66 cents a pound. Livestock futures are little changed, with live cattle falling 0.8% and lean hogs down 1.2%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1236 ET - Corn and soybean yields were trimmed by the USDA in the September WASDE report, but not by as much as expected by analysts. With corn yields forecast at 186.7 bushels an acre and soybeans at 53.5 bushels an acre, health issues reported from fields last month seemed to only have a minor impact on the figures. Some question if the USDA's projection is accurate. "Corn and bean yields higher than expected and well-above what early yield reports would suggest is likely," says Doug Bergman of RCM Alternatives in a note. After a wave of selling, corn and soybean futures on the CBOT have returned to positive territory, with corn up 0.1% and soybeans rising 0.8%. Wheat is down 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Blackstone Credit & Insurance Closed-End Funds Declare Monthly Distributions

The Funds' monthly distributions are set forth below. The following dates apply to the distribution declarations for the Funds:

The Funds declare a set of monthly distributions each quarter in amounts closely tied to the respective Fund's recent average monthly net income. As a result, the monthly distribution amounts for the Funds typically vary quarter-to-quarter, and shareholders of any Fund should not expect that Fund to continue to pay distributions in the same amounts shown above. The dynamic distribution strategy provides Blackstone Credit & Insurance with greater flexibility to maintain portfolio credit quality in varying market conditions. In addition, the dynamic distribution strategy reduces the need to retain reserves from net investment income to support the stability of future distributions.

A portion of each distribution may be treated as paid from sources other than net investment income, including but not limited to short-term capital gain, long-term capital gain, or return of capital. The final determination of the source and tax characteristics of these distributions will depend upon each Fund's investment experience during its fiscal year and will be made after the Fund's year end. Each Fund will send to investors a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year that will define how to report these distributions for federal income tax purposes.

Blackstone and Blackstone Credit & Insurance

Blackstone is the world's largest alternative asset manager. Blackstone seeks to deliver compelling returns for institutional and individual investors by strengthening the companies in which the firm invests. Blackstone's $1.2 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

Blackstone Credit & Insurance ("BXCI") is one of the world's leading credit investors. Our investments span the credit markets, including private investment grade, asset-based lending, public investment grade and high yield, sustainable resources, infrastructure debt, collateralized loan obligations, direct lending and opportunistic credit. We seek to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns for institutional and individual investors by offering companies capital needed to strengthen and grow their businesses. BXCI is also a leading provider of investment management services for insurers, helping those companies better deliver for policyholders through our world-class capabilities in investment grade private credit.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Hits Another Record
data.description:
Stocks in the US closed mixed on Friday, with the S&P 500 finishing just below the flatline, the Dow Jones slipping 273 points, and the Nasdaq 100 hitting fresh highs as investors interpreted soft jobs data and tame inflation as signals the Federal Reserve may cut interest rates next week.

The Nasdaq rose 0.4%, supported by a 6% surge in Tesla and a 2.1% gain in Microsoft after the tech giant avoided a potential EU antitrust fine, lifting the broader tech sector.

Tech and consumer discretionary stocks outperformed, while materials and health sectors lagged.

Traders are largely pricing in a 25-basis-point rate cut at the Fed’s September 17 meeting, with a small chance of a larger half-point cut, as easing appears likely given the economic data.

For the week, the S&P 500 has gained 1.6%, marking its best performance since early August, the Nasdaq has risen 2%, and the Dow is on track for its first weekly gain in three.

data.title: Western Alliance Bancorporation Announces $300 Million Share Repurchase Program >WAL
Thales, Airbus, Leonardo eye initial agreement on 10 bln-euro satellite JV, sources say
data.description:
Key points:
  • 'Project Bromo' seeks to counter competition from China, U.S
  • Negotiations have regained momentum after stalling, sources say
  • Political approval and final ownership details are still pending

Europe's aerospace groups Leonardo, Thales and Airbus have redoubled efforts to combine their satellite businesses into a 10 billion-euro ($11.7 billion), French-headquartered joint venture and are working towards hammering out an initial agreement in coming weeks, sources close to the matter said.

Under "Project Bromo," the three companies plan to set up a satellite manufacturing company to compete with rivals from China and the U.S., including Elon Musk's Starlink.

Talks have regained momentum after a rough period in the summer when the parties could not agree on governance and valuation, stalling an agreement, three sources said. The talks looked at risk of collapsing, according to another source.

A memorandum of understanding could now be signed by the end of September, though the timing could slip, the sources said.

Political signals so far have been encouraging, but details of ownership and governance must be signed by nations involved, three of the sources said. The venture is expected to include dedicated entities to protect sensitive national interests.

An agreement is not guaranteed, and talks could still fall apart, the sources warned.

The exact holdings of the new space venture are still being negotiated, the sources said, adding that ownership of the venture could be split roughly in three equal parts.

The new satellite venture value of about 10 billion euros reflects the units’ combined revenue of 6 billion-6.5 billion euros and sector peers, which trade at 1.5-3 times revenue, the two sources said.

Leonardo declined to comment.

Airbus referred to comments from CEO Guillaume Faury, who said earlier this week the companies were moving towards a deal and talking to European governments. “We are on the way,” he told a group of reporters in Washington.

“We have started also to communicate with all stakeholders and we have started to try for anti-trust (approval), so we are in the process of doing it,” Faury added.

Thales said: "No agreement has been reached at this stage. We are continuing our work. Any further comment would be premature."

Preliminary attempts over the past decade to create a European satellite champion foundered partly on anti-trust concerns and national rivalries.

But the dramatic rise of Starlink and a shift in the market towards cheaper, low-orbit satellites have increased pressure on Europe’s leading satellite manufacturers to combine assets or be pushed out of the market.

Talks to reshape the industry's structure are part of a broader attempt by Europe to boost sovereign capabilities as geopolitical tensions have mounted and U.S. policies have shifted.

All three companies have minority government shareholders and analysts say any agreement involving sensitive assets and technology would need political approval.

A person familiar with the matter said the venture was likely to be based in Toulouse, France, where Airbus is based, while noting such details were subject to final negotiations.

Decisions on where to place corporate power centres are often a sensitive issue in Europe’s fragmented aerospace sector.

Italy's industry ministry was not immediately available for comment. Germany's defense ministry did not immediately respond. France's state shareholding agency APE declined to comment.

A potential deal would create a standalone European satellite champion, based broadly on missile maker MBDA, owned by Airbus, Leonardo and BAE Systems.

MBDA was founded in 2001 through the merger of Anglo-French Matra BAe Dynamics, France's Aerospatiale Matra Missiles and missile activities of Anglo-Italian Alenia Marconi Systems.

Like MBDA, the new venture will include a mixture of unified cross-border activities with separate entities designed to protect sensitive national interests, the sources said.

($1 = 0.8536 euros)

data.title: Alliance Laundry Holdings Files for IPO
EGAIN Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
eGain Corp, a leading provider of customer engagement software solutions, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the competitive market for customer engagement software.

Financial Highlights

eGain Corp reported the following key financial metrics for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025:

  • Total Revenue: $88.4 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $92.8 million, primarily due to a decline in SaaS and Professional Services revenue.
  • Gross Profit: $62.0 million, indicating a slight decrease from $65.2 million in the prior year, driven by reduced revenue and stable cost of revenue.
  • Income from Operations: $4.4 million, down from $6.0 million in the previous year, as a result of increased research and development expenses.
  • Net Income: $32.3 million, a significant increase from $7.8 million in the prior year, largely due to a substantial tax benefit recognized during the period.
  • Basic EPS: $1.15, compared to $0.25 in the previous year, reflecting the increase in net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.13, up from $0.25 in the prior year, consistent with the rise in net income.

Business Highlights

eGain Corp's business performance for the fiscal year included several key operational highlights:

  • AI Knowledge Hub Solution: eGain automates customer experience with an AI knowledge hub solution, offering a comprehensive suite organized into three hubs: eGain AI Agent™, eGain AI Knowledge Hub™, and eGain Conversation Hub™ to enhance customer service.
  • Geographical Performance: North America accounted for 78% of total revenue, while Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) contributed 22% for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025.
  • Customer Segments: Over 87% of eGain's annual recurring cloud revenue for fiscal year 2025 came from large enterprises, defined as businesses with over a billion dollars in annual revenue or government organizations.
  • Competitive Strengths: eGain's solution is a comprehensive omnichannel platform with AI and knowledge applications at its core, offering enterprise-grade, secure cloud services with SOC2, PCI, HIPAA, FedRAMP, and GDPR certifications.
  • Partner Ecosystem: eGain has developed partnerships with system integrators and boutique consultants, and offers pre-built integrations with major platforms like Salesforce, Microsoft, and ServiceNow.
  • Future Outlook: eGain is investing in product and platform leadership, direct sales and marketing, and developing new partner relationships to accelerate growth. The company is also exploring strategic acquisitions to enhance its product portfolio.
  • Customer Support and Services: eGain provides 24/7 customer support via online and phone channels worldwide, with support centers in the United States, the United Kingdom, and India.
  • Research and Development: eGain is heavily investing in product innovation to capitalize on the global market opportunity for automating customer experience with AI.
  • Intellectual Property: As of June 30, 2025, eGain holds 17 issued patents in the United States, with expiration dates ranging from 2028 to 2040, underscoring its commitment to innovation and competitive advantage.

Strategic Initiatives

eGain Corp has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its market position and drive future growth:

  • Strategic Focus: eGain is focused on enhancing its strategic position by automating customer experience with an AI knowledge hub solution, targeting enterprises aiming to improve customer experience while reducing costs.
  • Capital Management: The company engaged in capital management activities including the repurchase of common stock, with $15.8 million spent on repurchasing shares in fiscal year 2025. eGain also issued common stock through employee stock purchase plans and stock option exercises, raising $1.4 million. The company maintained a strong cash position with $62.9 million in cash and cash equivalents as of June 30, 2025, and no long-term debt, indicating a focus on maintaining liquidity and financial flexibility.
  • Future Outlook: eGain plans to continue leveraging its AI knowledge hub to drive growth in SaaS revenue, while maintaining a strong cash position to support strategic initiatives. The company is evaluating the impact of new accounting standards on its financial statements, which may influence future capital management strategies.

Challenges and Risks

eGain Corp faces several challenges and risks in its operations and market environment:

  • Market Risks: The global economic climate, including tightening credit markets and volatility in financial markets, could negatively impact customer spending on technology, affecting the company's revenue.
  • Operational Risks: The company's SaaS business model is subject to risks such as subscription renewal rates, lengthy sales cycles, and reliance on a small number of significant customers. Additionally, the company faces challenges in expanding its sales force and maintaining strategic partnerships.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to evolving data privacy laws, such as the GDPR and CCPA, which could increase compliance costs and impact operations. The regulatory environment for AI is rapidly changing, potentially affecting the company's ability to offer AI-enabled solutions.
  • Emerging Risks: The integration of AI into products presents risks related to flawed algorithms and biased datasets, which could lead to reputational harm or legal liability. The company also faces cybersecurity threats that could compromise customer data and impact its reputation.
  • Foreign Currency Risks: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly in its EMEA operations, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, geopolitical conflicts and trade policy changes, such as tariffs, could impact the company's international operations and supply chain.

SEC Filing: EGAIN Corp [ EGAN ] - 10-K - Sep. 12, 2025

data.title: Champions Oncology Inc expected to post a loss of 1 cent a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Champions Oncology Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on September 15 for the period ending July 31 2025

  • The Hackensack New Jersey-based company is expected to report a 3.9% decrease in revenue to $13.513 million from $14.06 million a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Champions Oncology Inc is for a loss of 1 cent per share.

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy".

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Champions Oncology Inc is $12.00, about 46.2% above its last closing price of $6.46

data.title: Opendoor Is Sliding. Don't Call It a Meme Stock, Says New Chairman. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of Opendoor Technologies were falling sharply Friday as the online homebuying platform gave back some gains after its meteoric rise the day before.

The stock popped 80% on Thursday after the company tapped Shopify Chief Operating Officer Kaz Nejatian as its new CEO and reappointed co-founders Keith Rabois and Eric Wu to the board. The moves delighted the so-called Open Army, a loose collection of retail investors who have flooded into the stock over the last two months.

But shares fell 12% Friday, with some stockholders perhaps taking profits amid Opendoor's nearly 500% rise this year. Rabois' first television interview since his appointment as chairman didn't appear to reassure investors.

"It's not a meme stock," Rabois told CNBC's Sara Eisen on Friday, brushing off the idea that the stock's rise is down to hype alone. "Consumers are voting with their feet to say we want more capital being allocated to Opendoor."

The outspoken Khosla Ventures investor argued the company's previous leadership was unable to explain Opendoor's potential upside. The company has clear advantages in the online homebuying space, he said, and upgrading the team will help it reach this potential.

But the new leaders are inheriting a struggling business. Revenue is forecast to total $4.05 billion this year on roughly 10,800 homes sold, according to FactSet, down from $15.6 billion on 39,200 sales in 2022. The company has never posted a net profit over a fiscal year since going public in 2020.

Rabois said he didn't yet have access to Opendoor's financials and would start digging in and developing a strategy next week. "I'm sure we're presenting a pretty damn good plan before earnings," he added.

The company is expected to report third-quarter results in November.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Livestock Finish Day Lower — Market Talk
data.description: Livestock futures close down, with most-active live cattle futures finishing down 1% to $2.3105 a pound and lean hog futures closing down 1.1% to 98 cents a pound. Weaker export sales were reported for both pork and beef in yesterday's weekly export sales report — with beef sales down 27% from the previous week to 12,100 metric tons and pork sales down 27% to 17,300 tons. Weak export figures are one source of pressure for futures, says ADM Investor Services in a note. September's WASDE report from the USDA showed that the agency expects beef prices to continue rising heading into 2026, while pork prices also climb. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: CBOT soybeans tick up following USDA report
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures increased on Friday after the U.S. Department of Agriculture delivered a bearish, but unsurprising, monthly crop report.

  • The USDA projected U.S. yield at a slightly higher-than-expected 53.5 bushels per acre, compared with 53.6 bushels in August.

  • It pegged production at 4.301 billion bushels, up from 4.292 billion bushels a month earlier as the agency increased harvested acres.

  • The agency raised its U.S. soybean ending stocks forecast by 10 million bushels, but some analysts said the increase was lower than expected.

  • CBOT November soybeans (SX25) rose 12-3/4 cents to $10.46-1/4 a bushel.

  • CBOT December soymeal (SMZ25) settled $0.90 higher at $288.60 per short ton.

  • CBOT December soyoil (BOZ25) ended 0.57 cent higher at 52.17 cents per pound.

data.title: Bentley Systems, Incorporated Declares Third Quarter 2025 Dividend
data.description:
EXTON, Pa.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 12, 2025--

Bentley Systems, Incorporated , the infrastructure engineering software company, today announced that its Board of Directors declared a $0.07 per share dividend for the third quarter of 2025. The cash dividend is payable on September 30, 2025, to all stockholders of record of Class A and Class B common stock as of the close of business on September 23, 2025.

About Bentley Systems

Around the world, infrastructure professionals rely on software from Bentley Systems to help them design, build, and operate better and more resilient infrastructure for transportation, water, energy, cities, and more. Founded in 1984 by engineers for engineers, Bentley is the partner of choice for engineering firms and owner-operators worldwide, with software that spans engineering disciplines, industry sectors, and all phases of the infrastructure lifecycle. Through our digital twin solutions, we help infrastructure professionals unlock the value of their data to transform project delivery and asset performance.

This press release contains statements that are not historical in nature and that are intended to be, and are hereby identified as, "forward looking statements" as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including a statement regarding expectations as to payment of a quarterly cash dividend in the foreseeable future. Any future determination as to payment of dividends will depend upon the financial condition and results of operations of the company and such other factors as are deemed relevant by the board of directors. For example, macroeconomic conditions, pandemic consequences, a change in business needs including working capital, or a change in income tax law relating to dividends, could cause the company to decide not to pay a dividend in the future. A discussion of other risks and uncertainties is included in the company's filings with the SEC, including its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, and subsequent filings.

data.title: Nasdaq notches record high close, traders look to Fed meeting
data.description:
Key points:
  • US consumer sentiment slips again in September, survey says
  • Microsoft higher after deal to restructure OpenAI

The Nasdaq notched a record high close on Friday in a mixed trading session, lifted by Microsoft as investors looked ahead to the Federal Reserve's policy meeting next week, when it is widely expected to cut interest rates to counter a slowdown in the jobs market.

Lifted by Tesla and other technology-related stocks, the Nasdaq added to a rally in the previous session that saw all three indexes hit all-time highs.

Investors are laser-focused on the Fed's meeting on Tuesday and Wednesday. Traders expect the central bank to cut interest rates by 25 basis points after recent data showed longstanding weakness in hiring and easing inflation concerns.

"Because we had such a nice jump in the stock market yesterday, investors are basically catching their breath," said Sam Stovall, chief investment strategist CFRA Research. "There's really not going to be any data between now and Wednesday. It's a sort of wait-and-see attitude."

Microsoft gained after the technology giant avoided a possible hefty EU antitrust fine by offering customers reduced prices for Office products excluding Teams.

Tesla jumped after board chair Robyn Denholm dismissed concerns that CEO Elon Musk's political activity had hurt sales at the electric-vehicle maker and said the billionaire was "front and center" at the company after several months at the White House. With Friday's surge, Tesla shares remain down 2% in 2025.

Declines in Goldman Sachs and paint-maker Sherwin-Williams kept the Dow Jones Industrial Average in negative territory.

The University of Michigan's survey showed U.S. consumer sentiment fell for a second straight month in September as consumers saw rising risks to business conditions, the labor market and inflation.

According to preliminary data, the S&P 500 lost 3.47 points, or 0.05%, to end at 6,584.00 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 96.38 points, or 0.44%, to 22,139.46. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 273.44 points, or 0.59%, to 45,834.56.

Following signs of a worsening jobs market, interest rate futures reflect expectations of cuts totaling 75 basis points by the end of the year.

All three major indexes gained for the week, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq lifted by a revival in artificial intelligence trade after cloud computing giant Oracle's strong forecast on Tuesday.

Warner Bros Discovery extended a surge from Wednesday, when a source said Paramount Skydance was preparing a bid for the struggling media company.

Shares of vaccine makers fell after a report said U.S. health officials are planning to link coronavirus vaccines to the deaths of 25 children.
